episodes.title: Savitri and Satyavan — Mahabharata, Vana Parva

verses.english: A princess named Savitri, the auspicious daughter of King Ashvapati,
saw Satyavan in the forest hermitage, a man of pleasing appearance.

verses.iast: sa tam varayamasa pita uktva yamaya yasyatiti
verses.devanagari: सा तं वरयामास पिता उक्त्वा यमाय यास्यतीति
verses.english: She chose him as her husband, though her father warned that he was fated to go to Yama.

verses.iast: satyavan mriyate varshad ekena iti naradah praha
verses.devanagari: सत्यवान् म्रियते वर्षादेकेन इति नारदः प्राह
verses.english: Narada declared that Satyavan would die within one year.

verses.iast: tatha api sa tam eva varayamasa dridha-vrata
verses.english: Even so, she, firm in her vow, chose him alone.

verses.iast: varshante satyavaan patitah sa cha tasya shirassi upavishat
verses.devanagari: वर्षान्ते सत्यवान् पतितः सा च तस्य शिरसि उपाविशत्
verses.english: At the year's end, Satyavan fell; she sat cradling his head.
Yama came to take his life.

episodes.referential_question: In verse 7, 'tasya jivam' (his life) — whose life did Yama come to take?
candidate_options: Savitri (the princess, daughter of Ashvapati)
Satyavan (the husband who fell at the year's end)
Ashvapati (Savitri's father who gave the warning)
Narada (the sage who made the prophecy)
episodes.correct_answer: Satyavan (the husband who fell at the year's end)
episodes.explanation: Verse 6 establishes Satyavan patitah (Satyavan fell). Verse 7's tasya (his, genitive) refers to Satyavan as the most recent and most contextually prominent male antecedent. Narada and Ashvapati do not appear in verses 5-6.

episodes.title: Arjuna's Dilemma — Bhagavad Gita Chapter 1

verses.iast: arjunah uvaacha: drshtvedam svajanam krishna yuyutsum samupasthitam
verses.devanagari: अर्जुन उवाच: दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम्
verses.english: Arjuna said: Seeing these my own people, O Krishna, arrayed and eager for battle,

verses.iast: sidanti mama gatrani mukham cha parishushyati
verses.devanagari: सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति
verses.english: my limbs fail and my mouth is parched;

verses.iast: vepathu cha shareere me roma-harshah cha jayate
verses.english: my body quivers and my hair stands on end.
The Gandiva bow slips from my hand and my skin burns.

verses.iast: na cha shaknomyavasthatum bhramativa cha me manah
verses.devanagari: न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः
verses.english: I am unable to stand; my mind seems to whirl.
And I see ill omens, O Keshava (Krishna).

episodes.referential_question: In verse 4, 'Gandivam sramsate hastat' — from whose hand does the Gandiva slip?
candidate_options: Krishna (Keshava, Arjuna's charioteer and counsellor)
Arjuna (the warrior speaking throughout these verses)
Duryodhana (the leader of the opposing Kaurava army)
Bhishma (the elder warrior on the Kaurava side)
episodes.correct_answer: Arjuna (the warrior speaking throughout these verses)
episodes.explanation: The entire passage is Arjuna uvaacha (Arjuna said). All first-person references (mama, me, shareere me) refer to Arjuna. The Gandiva is Arjuna's divine bow. No antecedent shift occurs — the speaker throughout is Arjuna.

consistency_checkpoints.question: Whose hand (hastat) does the bow slip from?

episodes.title: Rama's Exile Decision — Ramayana, Ayodhya Kanda

verses.english: King Dasharatha ruled gloriously in Ayodhya.
He had four sons, beginning with Rama.

verses.iast: kaikeyii tu bharatasya mata ramam vanam praveshayitum ishte
verses.devanagari: कैकेयी तु भरतस्य माता रामं वनं प्रवेशयितुम् ईष्टे
verses.english: But Kaikeyi, Bharata's mother, desired to send Rama into the forest.

verses.iast: raja tu tam nivaaritum na shaknot varada-shrimitvaat
verses.devanagari: राजा तु तां निवारयितुं न शक्नोत् वरदश्रमित्वात्
verses.english: The king could not restrain her, being bound by his promise of boons.

verses.iast: ramah tu pituh vacanam shrutva vanam yatum nishchayam chakre
verses.devanagari: रामस्तु पितुः वचनं श्रुत्वा वनं यातुं निश्चयं चक्रे
verses.english: But Rama, hearing his father's words, resolved to go to the forest.

verses.iast: sita cha lakshmana cha tam anujagmatuh
verses.english: Sita and Lakshmana followed him.

episodes.referential_question: In verse 6, 'tam anujagmatuh' — whom did Sita and Lakshmana follow?
candidate_options: Dasharatha (the king who made the promise of boons)
Bharata (Kaikeyi's son for whose sake the exile was arranged)
Rama (who resolved to go to the forest in verse 5)
Kaikeyi (who desired to send Rama to the forest)
episodes.correct_answer: Rama (who resolved to go to the forest in verse 5)
episodes.explanation: Verse 5 ends with Rama as the subject resolving to depart. Verse 6's tam (accusative, him) refers to Rama — the most proximate and contextually salient male antecedent. The follow-up makes no sense for Dasharatha (who stays) or Kaikeyi (female).

consistency_checkpoints.question: Who resolved to go to the forest?

episodes.title: Chanakya's Teaching — Arthashastra

verses.iast: acharyo vishnuguptah chanakyanamah arthashastra rachanam chakre
verses.english: The teacher Vishnugupta, known as Chanakya, composed the Arthashastra.

verses.iast: sa chandragupta mauryam rajye sthaapayitum nandaan nashayitum chandrodayam akarot
verses.devanagari: स चन्द्रगुप्तं मौर्यं राज्ये स्थापयितुम् नन्दान् नाशयितुम् चन्द्रोदयमकरोत्
verses.english: He engineered the rise of Chandragupta Maurya to establish him on the throne and destroy the Nandas.
His maxims of statecraft became renowned among kings.

verses.iast: chandragupto api tasya upadesham shraddhaya agrihnat
verses.english: Even Chandragupta received his teachings with reverence.

verses.iast: iti tasya prabhavena maurya-samrajyam pratishtham aagamat
verses.english: Thus through his influence the Maurya Empire attained its stature.

episodes.referential_question: In verse 5, 'tasya prabhavena' (through his influence) — whose influence established the Maurya Empire?
candidate_options: Chandragupta Maurya (the king who was placed on the throne)
The Nandas (the dynasty that was destroyed)
Chanakya/Vishnugupta (the strategist teacher introduced in verse 1)
An unnamed minister referenced implicitly
episodes.correct_answer: Chanakya/Vishnugupta (the strategist teacher introduced in verse 1)
episodes.explanation: The consistent subject through verses 2-4 is Chanakya (sa in v2, tasya in v3, tasya upadesh in v4). Each tasya refers back to Chanakya from verse 1. Verse 5 continues this chain. Chandragupta is the object/recipient, not the agent of influence.

episodes.title: Gargi and Arjuna narrative session #1

verses.english: Gargi is introduced as daughter of Nakula.
She sees Arjuna in nagare.
She chooses him with her father's consent.
Satyavan warns his lifespan is short.
Even then she chooses him.
In time, Arjuna falls down.
Yama comes to take his life.

episodes.referential_question: In verse 7, whose life did Yama come to take?
candidate_options: Gargi (the chooser in the earlier verses)
Arjuna (the one who fell in verse 6)
Nakula (the father mentioned initially)
Satyavan (the prophet of verse 4)
episodes.correct_answer: Arjuna (the one who fell in verse 6)
episodes.explanation: The nearest antecedent in verse 6 is Arjuna, so 'tasya' in verse 7 resolves to Arjuna.

episodes.title: Vasistha teaches Rama — Ayodhya

verses.iast: acharyo vasistha sageh ayodhyae vasati sma
verses.english: The sage Vasistha dwelt at Ayodhya.

verses.english: There Rama, a king, approached and requested discipleship.

verses.iast: so apy enam anugrhiya shastra-margam bodhayamasa
verses.english: He graciously taught him the path of the sacred text.

verses.iast: rama api tasya upadeshena rajyam sushthutaya paalyamasa

An unnamed sage from a distant land

episodes.explanation: Verse 3 establishes Vasistha as the teacher; 'tasya' in v5 refers to Vasistha via the chain so(v3)->tasya(v4)->tasya(v5).
